characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Parents: We never learn about Edgar’s parents, but what we see of Gretchen, Jimmy, and Lindsay's respective parents is that all of them were emotionally abusive to some degree, which plays a huge role in why they are so dysfunctional in the first place. Big Eater: Gretchen, Lindsay, and Jimmy are all massive eaters, with Edgar serving as their resident chef. Sibling Rivalry: Once again with Gretchen, Jimmy, and Lindsay. Their relationships with their siblings range from nonexistent (Gretchen doesn't really talk to her brother, who has a family of his own) to outright antagonistic (both Jimmy and Lindsay have older sisters who are huge examples ofBig Sister Bully.) Ragtag Bunch of Misfits: A clinically depressed party girl, her antisocial author boyfriend, a sex maniac housewife, and aShell-Shocked Veteran.

characters.name: Jimmy Shive-Overly
characters.description: A selfish and insensitive British writer living in Los Angeles. Jimmy excuses his insensitive behavior as merely "telling it like it is", but it's clear he has a nicer side buried deep underneath. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Periodic moments between him and Gretchen prove that, for all their snark, their feelings for each other are very real and strong. Specifically, when hebuilds a pillow fort around miserable depressed Gretchen and stays instead of leaving her. British Stuffiness: Condescendingly critical, ego through the roof, and snarky as hell. He's definitely not sexually repressed, though. But for Me, It Was Tuesday: He's completely forgotten telling Becca "You and I have not made love for the last time" by the time she shows up at his house, sick of her husband and ready to confirm the prediction.Jimmy: That was quite the heckle. Calling the Old Man Out: Jimmy calls out Gretchen's parents in season 1 and then his own father in season 2.

characters.name: Gretchen Cutler
characters.description: A cynical and incredibly reckless music PR executive. Despite not being into relationships or monogamy, she decides to give things a shot with Jimmy when they become closer together. The Alleged Car: Her old Honda. We don't get a really good look at the whole thing in daylight until "Fix Me, Dummy", but it is impressively destroyed. Judging by what was visible in the flashback in "Constant Horror and Bone-Deep Dissatisfaction", it was an alleged car even before she crashed it. Anti-Hero: At her worst moments. Gretchen is frequently unsympathetic toward others' problems, snarky, dysfunctional to the extreme, and downrightviciouson occasion. Artistic License – Animal Care: In "Fists and Feet and Stuff", she equates herself and Jimmy to two pit bulls who would kill other dogs but are "couch buds" with each other because fighting would result in mutually assured destruction. In reality, if you have two dog-aggressive pits (or any breed), they absolutely will try to kill each other. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Gretchen and Jimmy are endlessly snarky, constantly deny and mock the concept of having feelings, and both react to Paul's definition of love (putting someone else's needs above your own) with "ew", but it just makes their sweet moments all the more heartwarming.

characters.name: Edgar Quintero
characters.description: Jimmy's housemate, an Iraq War veteran struggling with PTSD. Aborted Arc: His heroin addiction is never mentioned from Season 2 onward. Anti-Hero: In the sense of dysfunction rather than unpleasantness in the case of the two leads. Brutal Honesty: To Jimmy in the pilot, putting him in a headlock to force him to listen and admit that Gretchen stayed over and that means something. Again in "Constant Horror and Bone Deep Dissatisfaction", when he says that Jimmy has actually been kind of fun to be around with Gretchen, but if he's going to go back to 100% dick just so he can write another stupid book that Edgar won't read, Edgar's done with him.Not reading the book is the only part that gets a reaction from Jimmy. Butt-Monkey: PTSD, brief heroin addiction, homelessness, constant verbal abuse from Jimmy, inability to talk about his traumatic experience in Iraq because no one will listen, being strung along by Lindsay, meds interfering in intimacy once he actually does get a girlfriend, etc.

characters.name: Lindsay Jillian (nee Cottumaccio)
characters.description: Gretchen's loose best friend and enabler of her sex-drugs-and-rock-n-roll lifestyle and a newlywed who can't quite shed her selfish nature at the expense of her husband. Anti-Hero: As is standard for a character in a protagonist role on this show. Armor-Piercing Question: She realizes she's unhappy in her marriage when Shitstain and Honey Nutz are baffled by her having her jaw wired shut to lose weight. "Why did this fool buy the car if he don't even appreciate the features?" Babies Make Everything Better: Subverted to the point of deconstruction. She decides to have a baby in an attempt to save her marriage, but her public announcement that she wants to do so is met by Paul revealing his emotional affair and telling her he wants a divorce. In season 2, she finds out she actuallyispregnant but isn't going to keep it or tell Paul until Becca "accidentally" tells him and he dumps his new girlfriend to get back together with Lindsay.And then in "Try Real Hard", Lindsay gets so frustrated with Paul and his fussing that shestabs him. Eventually, shegets an abortion in "Talking To Me, Talking To Me" and leaves Paul for good. Big Eater: To the point that she gets her jaw wired shut in "What Normal People Do" in an attempt to lose weight and be skinny like the wives at Paul's firm.

characters.name: Paul Jillian
characters.description: Lindsay's husband, a nice if dorky guy who works for a bank as "Executive Vice President of Wealth Management", but he seems to have no duties to take up his time, spending it on odd hobbies. Beware the Nice Ones: Towards the end of "You Knew It Was a Snake", when Lindsay asks if he'll get rid of their prenup as they divorce, Paul simply replies that she "better lawyer up". Birds of a Feather: With Amy, a woman he was having an emotional affair with while still married to Lindsay. Endearingly Dorky: His relationship with Amy in season 2 is pretty cute, and apparently, his goofy personality was one of the things that attracted Lindsay to him. Evil Makeover: In Season 4, he gets one of these, with a slicked-back hairdo, new glasses, and a more slick wardrobe. He ditches part of it at the end of the season after realizing he's fallen into a pretty toxic crowd though.

characters.name: Becca Barbara (nee Cottumaccio)
characters.description: Lindsay's older sister and Jimmy's ex-girlfriend, Becca is image-obsessed to the point of neglecting her own happiness. The Alcoholic: Combined withAlcoholic Parent, she becomes one in Season 4,drowning her misery awayin copious amounts of booze. Fatal Flaw: She's almost pathologically selfish and self-centered. It is clear this is a family trait and we come to learn after meeting their mother that she and Lindsey both have aFreudian Excuse. The Glorious War of Sisterly Rivalry: With Lindsay.Word of Godsays that she makes certain decisions based on how much it'll tick her sister off. However,Becca still will come through for Lindsay when she needs the help, but not without somePassive-Aggressive Kombatfirst. Hypocrite: She yells at Lindsay for cheating on Paul, and then tries to sleep with Jimmy shortly afterward. In fact, as much as Becca claims to be mature and above it all, she's really just as (if not more) petty and self-absorbed as the others.

characters.name: Vernon Barbara
characters.description: Becca's immature husband, an orthopedic surgeon who "performs hip replacements for old ladies". Acronym and Abbreviation Overload: His standard way of speaking. Bumbling Dad: After Tallulah is born. Comically Missing the Point: Constantly.In the Pilot, while Jimmy is being thrown out of his and Becca's wedding:Jimmy: Enjoy your sham of a marriage!Vernon: I will!In "There is Not Currently a Problem", he excitedly asks Gretchen (who is in the throes of a manic-depressive meltdown) to give him a"The Reason You Suck" Speechand gets disappointed when she says he's not worth one.In "The Seventh Layer", he complains about how nothing exciting happens to him while Paul bemoans about the state of his marriage to Lindsay and how she's been indulging in cuckolding him. In the Pilot, while Jimmy is being thrown out of his and Becca's wedding:Jimmy: Enjoy your sham of a marriage!Vernon: I will!

characters.name: Killian Mounce
characters.description: Jimmy and Edgar's pre-teen neighbor who hangs out at Jimmy's house. Demoted to Extra: Makes fewer appearances over the course of the series, only appears once in Season 4, and has completely disappeared in Season 5. Minor Living Alone: His mother abandons the family and his father is barely around. Parental Abandonment: Hismotherleaves him and his dad. Parental Neglect: As of "Bad News: Dude's Dead", he's twelve (maybe thirteen) and basically living alone in his house as his dad justgets drunk all the time.

characters.name: Sam Dresden
characters.description: Gretchen's main client. Sam leads a rap trio alongside his friends Honeynutz and Shitstain and projects a "street" image, but his tastes run to Craft Movement furniture and fittings. Drama Queen: He builds a panic fort in his house after Honey Nutz and Shitstain record a track without him and drop it online. He's apparently built similar panic forts before, and Gretchen has to bribe him with food before he will venture outside again. Hair-Trigger Temper: Always seems tetchy and hot-headed. Hidden Depths: All over the place. He appreciates the classics, has extensive knowledge of ragtime music and its history, and watches a lot of foreign artsy movies. Impossibly Tacky Clothes: GQ Magazine called his style "courageously headachey". He describes it as a cross between skater kid and rave clothes.

characters.name: Ty Wyland
characters.description: A movie director who was Gretchen's lover before she met Jimmy, and who occasionally attempts to re-insert himself in her life, to Jimmy's dismay. Betty and Veronica: Subverted, as he's also a bit of a douche in several ways (just in a different manner from how Jimmy is). Lousy Lovers Are Losers: In thePilot Episode, as a contrast to how active and fun sex is with Jimmy for her, Gretchen is bored while having sex with Ty and tries to get out of it as soon as she can.

characters.name: Dorothy Durwood
characters.description: Edgar's girlfriend, whom he meets while working with an improv comedy troupe as part of his therapy for PTSD. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Is blonde and very kind and sweet. Giving Up the Dream: At the end of Season 3, shegives up on trying to make it as a comedy actress and moves out of California. Green-Eyed Monster: In "You Knew It Was a Snake",she reveals she's jealous at how Edgar's gotten much farther and become more successful than her in a faster span of time while she's been struggling for years trying to get decent work as an actress and has nothing to show for it. Hollywood Old:In-Universe- she's only in her early to mid-thirties, which is far from elderly, but in the youth-obsessed Hollywood, this is a virtual death sentence, with the only roles she's able to get being "overworked mom" and "kooky aunt" (if she gained some weight).

characters.name: Boone
characters.description: A friend of Ty's who befriends Gretchen. A divorcee, Gretchen begins hooking up with him to get back at Jimmy. Betty and Veronica: Played with. He's much more mature and stable than Jimmy, but he's not without his own issues. Good Parents: For all his own faults, he loves his daughter Olivia dearly and wants what's best for her. Hunk: The show finds all sorts of ways to get him shirtless. Romantic False Lead: What he ultimately turns out to be.

characters.name: Faye Cottumaccio
characters.description: Lindsay and Becca's mother, a former softcore porn actress. Freudian Excuse: For both Becca and Lindsay. Her constant negligence of them both led to their myriad of issues. Parental Neglect: Paid virtually no attention to her daughters, leaving them to raise themselves for the most part. Really Gets Around: She was a softcore porn actress and had a number of different lovers.

characters.name: The Shive-Overlys
characters.description: Jimmy's family, consisting of his father Ronnie, sisters Fiona, Di, and Lily, and an unnamed mother. Big Sister Bully: Fiona and Di were this to Jimmy and Lily, and still are in many respects. Lower-Class Lout: Ronnie works in a mine and thinks college is pretentious. Similarly, Fiona and Di aren't exactly class acts either. Lindsay lampshades this when she describes them as being like "Alabama English people". Missing Mom: Jimmy's mother left the family at some point to be with another man. White Sheep: Jimmy and Lily are the only half-decent members out of them.

characters.name: The Cutlers
characters.description: Gretchen's family, consisting of her father Fred, mother Vanessa, and an unnamed older brother. Freudian Excuse: Their extreme focus on putting on a good appearance and "keeping up with the Joneses" played a big part in why Gretchen acted out the way she did as a kid. It's All About Me: In "We Were Having Such A Nice Day", when Gretchencalls out her mother for never trying to understand her, Vanessa complains about how miserablesheis withherown life and how nothing she did was ever good enough.
